commits:
[{'sha': '4c23dd00ca0c9893beae386c176866a0f8cb26c7', 'message': 'Support @HttpExchange in MvcUriComponentsBuilder

MvcUriComponentsBuilder now recognizes @HttpExchange and composed
annotations such as @GetExchange in addition to @RequestMapping when
resolving class- and method-level mappings.

This allows controllers that implement @HttpExchange interfaces to use
fromController(), fromMethodName(), and fromMethodCall() to build links.

Closes gh-36235

Signed-off-by: Daeho Kwon <trewq231@naver.com>'}]
review_comments: ["Nice fix! Using `AnnotatedElementUtils` here seems right to make sure it picks up on the `httpExchange` mappings when they're defined on the parents interface which is how the docs suggest using them. Nice testing too especially on the `PersonHttpExchangeControllerImpl` to verify the inheritance works as expected. Also nice on  updating the error message to include `httpExchange` which will save time debugging."]

commits:
[{'sha': 'b01fdb01408499bf9c73de1e927017b4870ffe26', 'message': 'Fix ClassFileMethodMetadata return type names for primitives and arrays

Return correct names for primitive and array types
Introduce resolveTypeName helper for ClassDesc handling
Add ClassFileMethodMetadataTests (java24Test)
Extend AbstractMethodMetadataTests with void return case

See gh-36577

Signed-off-by: Junseo Bae <ferrater1013@gmail.com>'}, {'sha': '40ef6f68726521752ff030490eb9d658c97ee265', 'message': 'Fix method and annotation metadata for ClassFile variant

Prior to this commit, the ClassFile variant for  annotation and method
metadata would report incorrect metadata for:
* the method return type names in case of primitives and array types
* `toString` values for methods
* `equals` and `hashcode` information for methods

This commit expands the test suite and ensures that the ASM and
ClassFile variants are aligned.

Fixes gh-36577

Signed-off-by: Brian Clozel <brian.clozel@broadcom.com>'}]

commits:
[{'sha': '2508ca9bf0606eaa92b762cffe571434f7e2b5f0', 'message': "Augment FieldError.java's javadoc

to make clearer that defaultMessage is not a i18n key from your application bundle, but a static string.
Resulting in a problem that setting defaultMessage means no i18n. If you want i18n you should use the code parameter.

Behaviour shown in:
* https://github.com/ottlinger/custom-validation-messages-1029
* reported and resolved via https://github.com/thymeleaf/thymeleaf/issues/1029

Signed-off-by: P. Ottlinger <ottlinger@users.noreply.github.com>"}]
review_comments: ["Simple but effective change. By adding 'static' to the defaultMessage description it helps to prevent from accidentally passing translation keys into the wrong parameter.\n"]

    "description": "代码变更移除了类Javadoc中关于缓存机制的描述（'Previously resolved return types are cached for faster lookups.'）。然而，根据Spring Framework的代码历史，`HandlerMethodReturnValueHandlerComposite` 类确实在内部维护了一个 `Map<MethodParameter, HandlerMethodReturnValueHandler>` 作为缓存（例如 `this.returnValueHandlers` 映射或一个专门的 `resolvedHandlers` 缓存，具体实现可能因版本而异）。移除这条注释可能会误导开发者，使其认为该类没有缓存优化，从而影响对代码性能行为的理解。虽然注释可能不完全准确或过时，但直接删除而不提供更新后的说明会降低文档的清晰度。",
    "evidence": "@@ -28,7 +28,6 @@\n /**\n  * Handles method return values by delegating to a list of registered\n  * {@link HandlerMethodReturnValueHandler HandlerMethodReturnValueHandlers}.\n- * Previously resolved return types are cached for faster lookups.\n  *\n  * @author Rossen Stoyanchev\n  * @since 3.1",
    "advice": "建议核实当前类（`HandlerMethodReturnValueHandlerComposite`）是否确实存在缓存机制。如果存在，应更新Javadoc以准确描述缓存行为（例如，'Resolved handlers are cached internally for performance.'）。如果缓存机制已被移除，则此变更合理，但最好在提交信息或代码注释中简要说明移除原因，以保持文档的准确性。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

When the Accept-Language header is present but blank, defaultLocale
was ignored and fell back to Locale.getDefault() (JVM system locale).
A blank header represents the same intent as an absent header.

Closes gh-36513

review_comments: ['We consider the code in question fine as it is: we do not wrap concatenated strings within parentheses. If you are wondering about our coding guidelines, please consult our [Code Style](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/wiki/Code-Style) wiki page. In light of that, I am closing this PR. ---- In addition, your proposed change has absolutely nothing to do with "fixing a format string argument count". Did you perhaps use AI to generate this PR? ', 'I think this PR is valid, the parentheses do have a meaning here and are actually fixing a bug. In Java, the method call `.` operator has a higher precedence than the string concatenation `+` operator. Here, the arguments are only applied to the last string, before concatenation - so arguments are in fact not replaced. See the following: ```java System.out.println("First %s" + ", second %s" + ", third".formatted("1", "2")); // prints 'First %s, second %s, third' System.out.println(("First %s" + ", second %s" + ", third").formatted("1", "2")); // prints 'First 1, second 2, third' ``` Reopening.', 'Indeed. I apologize: I glanced at that too quickly.', ' @sbrannen I did not use AI. My test results are shown in the figure. In the original `str1 + str2.formatted(beanMethod, beanName)`, the `str2.formatted(beanMethod, beanName)` part is executed first, and then its result is concatenated with `str1`. 我没有使用AI 我的测试结果如图，原来的 `str1 + str2.formatted(beanMethod, beanName)`，其中 `str2.formatted(beanMethod, beanName)` 会先执行，然后其结果与 `str1` 进行连接', 'Thanks @cetf9h this is now merged and backported']

commits:
[{'sha': '3eca440c9848d81c7e3f60aead9be7f287678bda', 'message': 'Propagate max frame length to WebSocket session

This change ensures that maxFramePayloadLength from
WebsocketClientSpec is passed to ReactorNettyWebSocketSession.

Previously, the session used the default 64KB limit regardless
of client configuration, causing TooLongFrameException when
receiving larger frames from servers like Tomcat or Jetty.

Closes gh-36369

Signed-off-by: Artem Voronin <artem.voronin.dev@gmail.com>'}]
review_comments: ["For Reactor Netty, it looks like originally we had an `maxFramePayloadLength` field that was passed to `ReactorNettyWebSocketSession` that was then replaced in favour of the `WebSocketClientSpec` from Reactor Netty. However, the change 2ed281f6a89d50fe042335a1c88b515cb254afff to make the switch didn't pass the value into the session constructor, so that's a regression in 7.0. For Jetty, however, I'm not sure yet why we need to pass the settings from the Jetty `WebSocketClient` to the Jetty `Session`, with the latter created by the former. Could you drop the Jetty changes, and submit them as a separate PR, please?", 'Updated: While expanding the regression tests to cover all clients in `WebSocketIntegrationTests`, I discovered that `JettyWebSocketClient` suffers from the same issue. Even when the native Jetty client (org.eclipse.jetty.websocket.client.WebSocketClient) is configured with higher limits, the `JettyWebSocketSession` still defaults to 64KB, leading to: `CloseStatus[code=1009, reason=Text message too large: 69,624 > 65,536]` |Configuration|Log|State| |---|---|---| |`org.eclipse.jetty.websocket.client.WebSocketClient#setMaxFrameSize`, `org.eclipse.jetty.websocket.client.WebSocketClient#setMaxBinaryMessageSize`, `org.eclipse.jetty.websocket.client.WebSocketClient#setMaxTextMessageSize`, `org.eclipse.jetty.websocket.client.WebSocketClient#setMaxOutgoingFrames`||| ', 'After closer investigation, I’ve confirmed that `JettyWebSocketClient` correctly propagates the limits when properly configured, so I have restored `JettyWebSocketClient` to its original state. The initial issues I encountered were due to **local misconfiguration.**']

    "conclusion": "将泛型类型参数显式声明为可空，以提升类型安全性。",
    "description": "代码变更的主要目的是将 `HttpEntity`、`RequestEntity` 和 `ResponseEntity` 的泛型类型参数 `T` 显式声明为 `T extends @Nullable Object`。这旨在利用类型级别的空值注解（如 JSpecify 的 `@Nullable`）来更精确地表达类型参数的可空性，从而提升代码的类型安全性，并可能消除对单个字段或参数使用 `@Nullable` 注解的需要。然而，在 `HttpEntity` 类中，`body` 字段的类型从 `@Nullable T` 改为 `T`，但构造函数参数 `body` 的类型也从 `@Nullable T` 改为 `T`。这可能导致一个潜在问题：当 `T` 被实例化为非空类型（例如 `String`）时，构造函数现在要求一个非空的 `body` 参数，但类内部的无参构造函数和仅包含 `headers` 的构造函数仍然通过 `this(null, ...)` 传递 `null` 作为 `body`。这可能会与类型系统产生冲突，尽管使用了 `@SuppressWarnings(\"NullAway\")` 来抑制警告，但逻辑上可能存在不一致。",
    "evidence": "在 `HttpEntity.java` 中：\n1. 类声明改为 `public class HttpEntity<T extends @Nullable Object>`。\n2. `body` 字段从 `private final @Nullable T body;` 改为 `private final T body;`。\n3. 构造函数 `public HttpEntity(T body, @Nullable HttpHeaders headers)` 的参数 `body` 从 `@Nullable T` 改为 `T`。\n4. 无参构造函数 `protected HttpEntity()` 和单参数构造函数 `public HttpEntity(HttpHeaders headers)` 都调用了 `this(null, ...)`，并添加了 `@SuppressWarnings(\"NullAway\")`。",
    "advice": "建议重新审视设计：如果泛型 `T` 被声明为 `extends @Nullable Object`，这意味着 `T` 本身可以是可空类型。那么 `body` 字段的类型 `T` 应该能够容纳 `null` 值，因此将构造函数参数改为 `T` 是合理的。但是，需要确保所有调用构造函数并传递 `null` 的地方（例如无参构造函数）在类型检查上是安全的。目前通过 `@SuppressWarnings` 抑制了警告，这可能是可接受的，但应确保这种设计在整个框架中保持一致，并且不会导致运行时异常。另外，检查所有使用这些类的客户端代码，确保它们能正确处理类型变化。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },

    "description": "在多个构造函数上添加了 `@SuppressWarnings(\"NullAway\")` 注解，例如 `HttpEntity` 的无参构造函数、单 `HttpHeaders` 参数构造函数，以及 `RequestEntity` 和 `ResponseEntity` 的类似构造函数。这些注解用于抑制静态分析工具（如 NullAway）的警告，因为代码传递了 `null` 给可能期望非空值的参数。虽然这是必要的，但过多的抑制注解可能会降低代码的可读性，并可能掩盖潜在的空指针问题。",
    "evidence": "例如在 `HttpEntity.java` 中：\n`@SuppressWarnings(\"NullAway\")\nprotected HttpEntity() {\n    this(null, (HttpHeaders) null);\n}`\n以及 `@SuppressWarnings(\"NullAway\")\npublic HttpEntity(HttpHeaders headers) {\n    this(null, headers);\n}`",

    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

commits:
[{'sha': 'ffd8f2ee3a6df14439b4a482ba1b57378c3e8c93', 'message': 'Use Java Files utilities in FileCopyUtils

Use helpers from java.nio.file.Files for some methods in FileCopyUtils.

Additionally, add unit tests for the various file-related methods.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Strawderman <pstrawderman@netflix.com>'}]
review_comments:
["@jhoeller should we deprecate some of those utility methods as a result? We've done that in the past when Java upgrades turn those into one-liners.", "For completeness, I've also applied consistent stream closing through try-with-resources in #36223 and aligned 6.2.16 in #36224.

As for deprecating some methods, most of them do not provide much additional anymore indeed. However, they are quite old and potentially called from many parts of the ecosystem, so I'd rather leave them in place - not least of it all, for their consistent closing of streams in `FileCopyUtils` and the flushing after copying in `StreamUtils` (which some callers may effectively rely on).

FWIW, once we have a JDK 25 baseline, we can use `Reader#readAllAsString` in our `FileCopyUtils#copyToString` implementation."]

commits:
[{'sha': '9bc980cd85e6d326d3a3312bec36940e31893541', 'message': 'Polish RetryTask

1. Fix wrong type name in assertion message.
2. Rename `retryTemplate` to `retryOperations` to match type better.

Signed-off-by: Yanming Zhou <zhouyanming@gmail.com>'}]
review_comments: ['Well spotted - however, while this is debatable, it is actually intentional. `JdbcClient` and `JmsClient` follow the same pattern of accepting `Operations` interfaces while suggesting the `Template` classes both in the parameter name and the assertion message. The rationale is that the common thing to pass in there is a template instance, with the operations interface primarily declared for testability. In other words, the operations interface is just a facade for a specific template rather than an independent contract.']
